title: Valid Parentheses
code: // c is a closer: top must be its matching opener
return st.Count == 0; // any leftover opener => invalid

frames.caption: Set up the close→open map and an empty stack, then scan the string left to right.
frames.note: The map lets a closing bracket name exactly which opener it requires: ')' demands '(', ']' demands '[', '}' demands '{'. The stack remembers, in order, every opener still waiting to be closed. With those two pieces, one linear pass decides validity — O(n) time, O(n) space for the stack (worst case "(((((" pushes everything).

frames.caption: '(' is an opener → push it onto the stack.
frames.note: An opener has no obligation yet — we just record that it is now the most recent thing waiting to be closed, so we push it. A stack is exactly right here: nesting is last-in-first-out. The innermost (most recently opened) bracket must be the first one closed, which is precisely the order a stack hands things back.

frames.caption: '[' is an opener → push it onto the stack.
frames.note: An opener has no obligation yet — we just record that it is now the most recent thing waiting to be closed, so we push it. A stack is exactly right here: nesting is last-in-first-out. The innermost (most recently opened) bracket must be the first one closed, which is precisely the order a stack hands things back.

frames.caption: '{' is an opener → push it onto the stack.
frames.note: An opener has no obligation yet — we just record that it is now the most recent thing waiting to be closed, so we push it. A stack is exactly right here: nesting is last-in-first-out. The innermost (most recently opened) bracket must be the first one closed, which is precisely the order a stack hands things back.

frames.caption: '}' is a closer; it demands opener '{'. The top of the stack must equal '{'.
frames.note: TryGetValue on the close map both classifies the char (in the map ⇒ it is a closer) and tells us the opener we need. A closer can only be matched by the most recently pushed opener, so the very next thing we check is the stack top.

frames.caption: Pop '{'. It equals the required '{' → matched pair. Continue.
frames.note: A closer must be answered by the most recent opener, so we pop the top and check it equals the opener this closer demands (via the close→open map). LIFO guarantees the popped item is the innermost unmatched opener — exactly the one that has to close first. A match means this pair nests correctly; we move on.

frames.caption: ']' is a closer; it demands opener '['. The top of the stack must equal '['.
frames.note: TryGetValue on the close map both classifies the char (in the map ⇒ it is a closer) and tells us the opener we need. A closer can only be matched by the most recently pushed opener, so the very next thing we check is the stack top.

frames.caption: Pop '['. It equals the required '[' → matched pair. Continue.
frames.note: A closer must be answered by the most recent opener, so we pop the top and check it equals the opener this closer demands (via the close→open map). LIFO guarantees the popped item is the innermost unmatched opener — exactly the one that has to close first. A match means this pair nests correctly; we move on.

frames.caption: ')' is a closer; it demands opener '('. The top of the stack must equal '('.
frames.note: TryGetValue on the close map both classifies the char (in the map ⇒ it is a closer) and tells us the opener we need. A closer can only be matched by the most recently pushed opener, so the very next thing we check is the stack top.

frames.caption: Pop '('. It equals the required '(' → matched pair. Continue.
frames.note: A closer must be answered by the most recent opener, so we pop the top and check it equals the opener this closer demands (via the close→open map). LIFO guarantees the popped item is the innermost unmatched opener — exactly the one that has to close first. A match means this pair nests correctly; we move on.

frames.caption: Scan complete and the stack is empty → return true.
frames.note: Third failure mode lives here: leftover openers (a non-empty stack) mean some bracket was never closed, so the answer would be false. For "([{}])" every opener was popped by its matching closer, so the stack ends empty → VALID. Each char was processed once, confirming the O(n) bound.
